heading: Release of trade secrets
label: 2157 Release of trade secrets
notes: Effective DateSection effective one year after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1985 Amendment note under .Dec. 23, 1985section 1759 of Pub. L. 99–198section 2131 of this title
amendments: Effective DateSection effective one year after , see , set out as an Effective Date of 1985 Amendment note under .Dec. 23, 1985section 1759 of Pub. L. 99–198section 2131 of this title

children.heading: Release of confidential information prohibited
children.label: a Release of confidential information prohibited

children.text: the trade secrets, processes, operations, style of work, or apparatus; or
the identity, confidential statistical data, amount or source of any income, profits, losses, or expenditures,

children.heading: Wrongful use of confidential information prohibited
children.label: b Wrongful use of confidential information prohibited

children.text: to use or attempt to use to his advantages; or
to reveal to any other person,

children.label: A
children.text: a fine of not more than $1,000 and imprisonment of not more than one year; or

children.label: B
children.text: if such violation is willful, a fine of not more than $10,000 and imprisonment of not more than three years.

children.heading: Recovery of damages by injured person; costs; attorney’s fee
children.label: d Recovery of damages by injured person; costs; attorney’s fee
children.text: Any person, including any research facility, injured in its business or property by reason of a violation of this section may recover all actual and consequential damages sustained by such person and the cost of the suit including a reasonable attorney’s fee.

children.heading: Other rights and remedies
children.label: e Other rights and remedies
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect any other rights of a person injured in its business or property by reason of a violation of this section. Subsection (d) shall not be construed to limit the exercise of any such rights arising out of or relating to a violation of subsections (a) and (b).
